                                                                                  CALENDAR OF FEATURES
                                                           In addition to the monthly data appearing regularly in Employment and
                                                           Earnings, special features appear in most of the issues, as shown below:

                                                                                         Jan. Feb;    Mar. Apr.    May   July   Aug. Sept. Oct.   Nov.
                                      Household data
                                    Annual averages                                       X

                                    Revised seasonally adjusted series and
                                     current seasonal factors                                    X
                                    Quarterly averages:
                                     Seasonally adjusted data
                                     Persons not in labor force                           X                   X            X                X
                                     Vietnam Era war veterans
                                      Establishment data

                                  Industry divisions (preliminary)                     X

                                  Industry detail (final)                                           X

                                 Women employment (National)                                  X                X             X                 X

                                 National data adjusted to new benchmarks                                                         (1)
                                 Revised seasonally adjusted series and
                                  current seasonal factors                                                                        (D
                                 State and area annual averages                                                X

                                 Area definitions                                                              X
                                    1
                                       The issue that introduces the establishment data adjusted to new benchmarks varies. The September 1971 issue
                                marks the introduction of March 1970 benchmarks.

                                                  October 1971
                    Total employment continued to rise in October, and        month, from 8.0 to 7.2 percent, following increases in
                 unemployment edged down. The unemployment rate               the previous 2 months. The reduction in blue-collar

                          War veterans are defined by the dates of their service in the United States Armed Forces. War veterans 20 to 29 years old are
                  all veterans of the Vietnam Era (service at any time after Aug. 4, 1964), and they account for about 85 percent of the Vietnam
                  Era veterans of ail ages. About 600,000 post-Korean-peacetime veterans 20 to 29 years old are not included in this table.

                       "    Initial inclusion in this publication. Subarea of Philadelphia, Pennsylvania Standard Metropolitan Statistical Area: Philadelphia County.
                       "    Total includes data for industry divisions not shown separately. Services excludes agriculture, forestry, and fisheries.
                            Subarea of Washington, D.C. Standard Metropolitan Statistical Area: Alexandria, Fairfax, and Falls Church cities and Arlington, Fairfax, Loudoun, and Prince William Counties Virginia
                       *    Not available.

                     Job vacancies are the stock of unfilled job openings as of the
                 close of the last business day of the reference month. Openings
                 for all kinds of positions, classifications and employment, full       Size and regional stratification

                                                                          ATTENTION

                    As discussed in the Technical Note, the Bureau periodically          Employment and Earnings, the national data in Sections, B, C,
                adjusts the industry employment series to a recent benchmark             and D supersede those published in previous issues, as well as
                to improve their accuracy. These adjustments may also affect             those appearing in the Handbook of Labor Statistics, 1971.
                the hours, earnings, job vacancy, and labor turnover series be-
                                                                                         Comparable data will be published in Employment and Earnings,
                cause employment levels are used as weights. Industry data for
                all national series shown in this report have been adjusted to           United States, 1909-71, BLS Bulletin 1312-8.
                March 1970 benchmarks. Data from April 1970 forward are                       Beginning with July 1 9 7 1 , job vacancy data have been ad-
                subject to revision at the time of the next benchmark.                    justed to reflect the effects of March 1970 benchmark employ-
                    Beginning with the September 1971 and subsequent issues of            ment levels and are not strictly comparable with those of earlier
                                                                                          months.
